subject: [RFC PATCH 43/45] mm: page_alloc: trigger defrag from allocator hot path on tainted-SPB pressure

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch adds two triggers to the page allocator's hot path to start defragmentation on tainted sub-page blocks (SPBs) that are under pressure. The first trigger fires when a successful allocation already evaluates the SPB's shrink threshold, and the second trigger walks the tainted-SPB list before fragmenting the clean pool. These triggers aim to catch stuck SPBs with no allocator activity to drive defragmentation, while still respecting the cooldown gate to prevent storming the worker. The patch also centralizes the reaction to tainted allocations into a helper function.
